content: I will now write the `walkthrough.md` file summarizing the changes made to transition the app queries to `website_pooja_products`.

args.ArtifactMetadata: {"ArtifactType":"walkthrough","RequestFeedback":false,"Summary":"Walkthrough documenting changes for migrating the mobile app database queries to website_pooja_products with support for structured data formatting (JSON arrays, text arrays) and dynamic categories."}
args.CodeContent:
"# Walkthrough - Database Migration to `website_pooja_products`

We have successfully migrated the mobile app storefront from the legacy `general_poojas` table to the new `website_pooja_products` table. The application now builds and compiles cleanly without any TypeScript errors.

## Changes Made

### 1. Shop Tab Screen (`app/(tabs)/puja.tsx`)
- Changed query table name to `website_pooja_products`.
- Set filter to `.eq('is_published', true)` matching the new table column.
- Mapped fields:
  - `p.name` -> `title`
  - `originalPrice` -> formatted with currency symbol `₹`
  - `price` -> formatted with currency symbol `₹`
  - `rating` -> cast to string
  - `reviews_count` -> cast to string
  - `temple_association` -> `provider`
  - `duration` -> `time`
  - `image` -> `image` (resolving the public R2 image URL)
- Subscribed the realtime listener channel to `website_pooja_products`.

### 2. View All Screen (`app/all_pujas.tsx`)
- Changed query table name to `website_pooja_products`.
- Set filter to `.eq('is_published', true)`.
- Mapped and formatted fields correctly.
- Replaced the static `CATEGORIES` array filter with a **dynamic category selector**:
  - Dynamically extracts all unique categories from the loaded database products.
  - This ensures new product categories (like `Rudraksha` and `Bracelet`) display as category filter tabs automatically.
- Subscribed the realtime listener channel to `website_pooja_products`.

### 3. Product Detail Page (`app/puja_detail.tsx`)
- Changed the fallback database search query table to `website_pooja_products`.
- Implemented smart mapping to format complex database columns into plain text for the UI:
  - **`benefits`**: Maps string arrays to bullet points (`\n` separated).
  - **`steps`**: Formats the `rituals_included` JSON array (`name`, `duration`, `description`) into a step list.
  - **`samagri`**: Formats the `samagri_list` JSON array (`name`, `quantity`) into a comma-separated list of materials.
  - **`pandit`**: Formats the `priest_
